title: Accessibility-first design face for a non-Latin community script
context: This backlog task combines both program goals: apply the accessibility-first design principles from the Latin family (M1) to a non-Latin community-co-designed script (M2 pattern). It requires a secured script community partner, a completed accessibility evidence base, and significant expert co-ordination. This is advanced, high-coordination work combining the most complex elements of the program; it is appropriately deferred until both the Latin and first script families are proven.
objective: Co-design with a community partner an accessibility-first type family for a non-Latin script, applying published legibility principles to the script's forms with community review, lived-experience review from readers of that script, and honest non-medical framing.
acceptanceCriteria: A named community partner for the target script is secured in writing before any work begins; verifiedNeed remains false until then.
Accessibility specialists and lived-experience reviewers (users of the target script with low vision or relevant reading differences) are secured and co-sign the design brief.
Design applies legibility principles (where applicable from published research or community-specified need) adapted for the specific visual structure of the script.
Non-medical framing text is applied; no medical or clinical efficacy claims made.
All M2 criteria (co-signed brief, glyph drawing, HarfBuzz clean, native-reader review, OFL release with provenance) are met.
At least one adoption event: community use confirmed or upstream PR initiated.
output: A pull request and tagged release for an accessibility-first non-Latin script font family, co-designed with its community, with specialist and lived-experience sign-off, passing CI gates, complete OFL/provenance artifacts, and at least one adoption event.
